geography.data_source: KMC ward map 2022, North Kolkata traditional zone boundary

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 Kolkata district tables (North zone wards), marginal population decline at -0.2% CAGR in old city wards

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round (2020-21) West Bengal urban tables, PLFS 2022-23, North Kolkata traditional employment patterns — artisan and small manufacturing heritage

commute_profile.data_source: Kolkata Metro Railway ridership 2023, CSTC bus data, CTC tram data, narrow lane accessibility limits car usage

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Estimated from traditional middle-income zone with very high political mobilisation — North Kolkata is historically Bengal's political protest epicentre, para (neighbourhood) culture drives rapid diffusion

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from North Kolkata's para (neighbourhood) culture — extremely tight community networks, Durga Puja committee networks, addas (gathering spaces) as information hubs

informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from Shyambazar five-point crossing market area, KMC market survey
Estimated from Bagbazar-Kumartuli idol-making artisan cluster, traditional craft industry
Estimated from Hatibagan market area commercial activity and theatre district

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: Adjusted Middle Income Residential template for North Kolkata's traditional character — elevated retired weight for older demographic, increased small_business for artisan/trader concentration

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from KMC ward councillor count, strong Durga Puja committee / cultural organisation network (unique to Kolkata), TMC/CPIM political structures
